_note: estimated — run `go test -bench=. -benchmem -benchtime=3s ./...` for real numbers on your hardware

results.description: Lamport clock increment throughput (mutex lock + increment + return)
results.note: bounded by mutex acquisition; lockless atomic would be ~2× faster

results.description: Full Lamport send+receive cycle (two mutex acquisitions)
Vector clock tick with 5-node vector (no map copy)
results.note: map access is ~5× slower than Lamport's single counter

results.description: Vector clock send with 5-node vector (tick + full map copy)
results.note: map copy adds ~75ns over tick alone

results.description: HLC timestamp generation (mutex + wall clock read + counter logic)
results.note: time.Now().UnixMilli() dominates; ~60ns per call on M2

results.description: Happens-before comparison on 5-element vector clocks (pure computation)
results.note: no locks; map iteration over 5 keys is very fast

results.description: Concurrent event detection on 5-element vector clocks
results.note: two happens-before checks; 2× cost of single check as expected

results.description: False positive rate for non-concurrent events (should be 0%)
results.note: vector clocks are exact — no false positives possible

results.description: Detection rate for genuinely concurrent events (should be 100%)
results.note: vector clocks capture concurrency exactly — no false negatives possible
